title: Snake gourd — wide spacing + organic-rich soil for a 4-month 20 t/ha crop
body: HORDI plants snake gourd (Trichosanthes cucumerina L.) at 1.5 × 1.5 m — noticeably wider than most vining cucurbits — because the pendant fruit needs drop space and the vine is aggressive. Soil pH 5.5–7.5 with 10 t/ha of well-decomposed organic matter incorporated pre-plant; seed rate 4 kg/ha. Basal fertilizer is 75 Urea + 200 TSP + 60 MOP (kg/ha), then two symmetric top-dressings of 75 Urea + 60 MOP at 4 and 8 weeks. Harvest starts 60–75 days after planting and continues every 4 days. Released varieties (TA-2, MI-Short, Thinnaweli) all target a 20 t/ha commercial yield.
quote: Spacing: 1.5 m x 1.5 m. Seed Rate: 4kg/ha. Basal: Urea 75, TSP 200, MOP 60. Top Dressing (4 weeks): Urea 75, MOP 60. Top Dressing (8 weeks): Urea 75, MOP 60. Add 10 t/ha of organic matters.
